events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. Sequence check torque & threaded fastening. Observed field condition: torque sequence pattern violated (spiral/star from center - SEQ). Work actively in progress. Need evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue.

We need decision fail because non-compliant sequence violation. Risk high? Torque sequence violation can cause uneven clamping, gasket distortion, bolt overload/underload, leak or structural failure. In production floor quality gates. So risk high. Work_stage in-progress. Component_conditions installed-defective because installed but sequence violated (defective installation). percent_complete maybe 70? Value not asked numeric reading; use 0. sound_source etc default empty? Schema says string for sound_source, numbers for event_time, rate. We can set sound_source "" and event_time 0.0, rate 0.0. order list of step-ids? The task asks sequence; we can include proper order e.g. ["clean-threads", "inspect-fasteners", "install-hardware", "hand-snug", "torque-to-spec-in-sequence", "verify-mark", "quality-sign-off"]. workable maybe activities that can still start: inspection, rework? Need decide.

Findings: torque sequence pattern violated; fasteners not loaded uniformly; potential for gasket damage / warped flange / clamp load variation. Actions: stop work, loosen all fasteners to hand-tight/snug, re-torque per OEM specified spiral/star pattern from center, verify torque and witness marks, re-inspect. Rationale: proper torque sequence ensures uniform clamp load; deviation is non-compliant and must be corrected before acceptance. References: OEM/engineering specs, maybe ASME PCC-1? But reference material says OEM/engineering specs. Could include "OEM torque specification / engineering drawing".

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside JSON. We must write to /app/answer.json? In this environment, we can output JSON content. The instruction says write valid JSON to /app/answer.json. As assistant, we can provide JSON. Since no file system tool, likely just output JSON. But maybe we should include only JSON. Need no markdown code fences? The user says "Return exactly one valid JSON object matching the requested answer schema. Do not include prose, Markdown, code fences, or commentary outside JSON." So final should be raw JSON only. Good.

Need fill all fields. component_conditions: ["installed-defective"] maybe also "non-compliant"? The categories list includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. The violation is non-compliant. But component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective. Could include both installed-defective and non-compliant. The scenario says condition categories that may apply: installed-defective. It might expect only installed-defective. But non-compliant also clearly applies. However the provided allowed categories include non-compliant. Use ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"]? Need decide. The prompt says "Component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective" maybe hint. But including non-compliant is accurate. Could include both. Risk high.

percent_complete: estimate overall progress. Since torque sequence violated during final torquing, maybe 80%. value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0.0 rate 0.0 sound_source "".

order: maybe list step IDs for correct sequence. Since observed violation is spiral/star from center - SEQ, the correct order is that pattern. Could include ["torque-center", "torque-spiral-star-outward", "verify-torque", "mark-fasteners"]. But task asks evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue. order field maybe for audio/video tasks; but we can include correct sequence. Use generic step IDs.

workable: list activity IDs that can still start. Since work must stop and rework, maybe ["rework-torque-sequence", "re-inspection"]. Or if asking what work can still start after correction? Hmm. The field description: "Us…
